title: Inventory event processor bottleneck starves order and catalog systems
description: Inventory service event processing stream falls behind, creating cascading backlog through order confirmations and catalog stock updates, reaching API gateway users.

logs.message: Event stream processing: 1250 events/sec ingested, 890 events/sec processed
Consumed offset lag: 2.3 minutes behind latest committed offset
Processing latency p99: 2100ms for stock mutation events (baseline 350ms)
Batched read of 250 SKU records from database completed successfully
Stock reservation confirmation timeout from inventory: 18% of order confirmations delayed >4s
Order confirmation publishing queue: 567 messages waiting for acknowledgment (normal: 40)
Stock sync events queued: 1204 pending updates unable to commit
Product availability stale: max 8 minutes out of sync for 34% of active SKUs
GET /inventory/:sku returned 503 (Service Unavailable): catalog-service sync queue overloaded
POST /orders returned 503 (Service Unavailable): order confirmation publishing backlog
Order confirmation email queue backlog: 2100 pending messages
Email send throughput: 12 emails/min (baseline 280 emails/min), 95% reduction
Daily sync job started: data warehouse snapshot scheduled
CPU throttling detected: workload exceeding allocation, processing falling further behind

alerts.message: Inventory event stream processing lag exceeding SLA with increasing offset divergence
Order service confirmation latency spike due to inventory dependency backlog
Catalog service stock sync queue overflowing and data freshness degraded
API gateway 503 errors across inventory and order endpoints
Notification service queue not keeping pace with message production
Data warehouse sync job execution started

ground_truth.correct_fix: Scale event processor consumer group: increase num.stream.threads=8; add more consumer instances; optimize event processing logic; check Kafka partition rebalancing; kubectl scale deploy/inventory-service --replicas=3
